old_claim.note: Cartridge Ammunition provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The Maxim gun's automatic cycle depended on spent cartridges being ejected and fresh cartridges being drawn into the action.

source_shape.source_locator: The Power of Recoil section: PBS says the recoil force ejected the used cartridge and drew in the next one, allowing the one-barrel gun to fire automatically.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: PBS describes Maxim's automatic operation in terms of ejecting one cartridge and drawing in the next cartridge.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That firing cycle depends on self-contained cartridge ammunition because the machine action handles spent and fresh cartridges as discrete ammunition units.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: machine_gun remains enabled by cartridge_ammunition, now with source-backed evidence.
would_reject_if: The machine_gun node is later narrowed to non-cartridge automatic guns.
A stronger source shows Maxim's first effective gun did not use cartridge ammunition.
The cartridge_ammunition node is narrowed away from self-contained rounds handled by firearm actions.
short_reason: Maxim's automatic cycle handled spent and fresh cartridges.
